tests.name: loadManifest loads the shared fixture manifest
loadManifest rejects a manifest that fails to parse as JSON
loadManifest rejects an unsupported manifest version
loadManifest rejects an empty case list
loadManifest rejects a case referencing an unknown command
loadManifest rejects a case whose args is not an array of strings
loadManifest rejects duplicate case ids
loadManifest rejects a fixture path that escapes the shared fixture root
loadManifest rejects a missing expected reference
loadManifest rejects a host-specific extra field on a case (no per-host fixture/golden paths)
loadDescriptor rejects a descriptor that fails to parse as JSON
loadDescriptor loads both host descriptors
loadDescriptor rejects unknown version
loadDescriptor rejects unknown host
loadDescriptor rejects unknown invocation
loadDescriptor rejects unknown display
loadDescriptor rejects a non-relative entry path
loadDescriptor rejects an entry path that traverses above the templates root
checkAdapterBoundary passes on the real descriptors and templates (no embedded rule ids)
checkAdapterBoundary fails a template planted with an embedded kernel rule id
checkAdapterBoundary fails a template planted with a lower-case kernel rule id (case-insensitive match)
normalizeObservation parses structured JSON stdout and slash-normalizes diagnostic paths
normalizeObservation falls back to the trimmed raw string when stdout looks like JSON but fails to parse
normalizeObservation parses a top-level JSON object (not array) without mapping over it
normalizeObservation keeps non-JSON stdout as a trimmed string
baselineHash returns empty string for a root that does not exist
baselineHash walks nested directories and includes each file and directory entry
baselineHash changes when a file is added and reverts when removed (used as the rollback baseline check)
installAdapter / rollbackAdapter installs the descriptor entry into the consumer root and rolls back to baseline
installAdapter / rollbackAdapter does not remove a pre-existing directory during rollback (only removes directories it created)
installAdapter / rollbackAdapter rejects a hand-built descriptor whose entry escapes the host templates root (defense in depth, bypassing loadDescriptor)
compareObservations reports no differences for identical observations across hosts
compareObservations detects a mutated exit code between hosts
compareObservations detects a mutated diagnostic field between hosts
runConformance (full parity suite, in-process) runs every fixture case through both hosts with identical normalized results, and cleans up after itself
runConformance (full parity suite, in-process) overall exit code matches the worst case across the suite (0/1/2)
runConformance (full parity suite, in-process) exits 2 and never runs parity comparison when both descriptors share the same host
runConformance (full parity suite, in-process) exits 2 for an empty case list rather than silently reporting success
runCli (in-process, exercises every branch Stryker can attribute to this file) exits 0 with empty JSON diagnostics for the full fixture suite
runCli (in-process, exercises every branch Stryker can attribute to this file) exits 0 with empty text output (no trailing newline noise) in text format
runCli (in-process, exercises every branch Stryker can attribute to this file) exits 2 and reports a boundary violation as text when format is not json
runCli (in-process, exercises every branch Stryker can attribute to this file) exits 2 via the catch branch when --manifest points at a nonexistent file
runCli (in-process, exercises every branch Stryker can attribute to this file) rejects an unknown CLI argument (in-process)
runCli (in-process, exercises every branch Stryker can attribute to this file) rejects missing required arguments (in-process)
pl-adapter-conformance CLI (subprocess smoke tests) exits 0 for the full fixture suite with no parity or boundary violations
pl-adapter-conformance CLI (subprocess smoke tests) exits 2 and reports a boundary violation for a planted rule-id template
pl-adapter-conformance CLI (subprocess smoke tests) rejects an unknown CLI argument
fixture installation stays isolated to temporary roots (repo scope guard) never writes outside the provided consumer root
ts-pl host-neutral skill keeps command, diagnostic, rule-id, and activation contracts byte-identical
ts-pl host-neutral skill ships all kernel references, including host-adapter parity, without unrelated Phase-B integration instructions
ts-pl host-neutral skill host-adapters reference states fixture-only scope and no real-host/dogfood claim
